hypothesis: If a reinforcement learning generative model is rewarded to maximize CATS-based pharmacophoric similarity to known estrogen receptor antagonists/degraders while penalizing MACCS/MAP4 structural similarity and maintaining QED, then it will produce molecules with significantly better QVina docking scores to ER (PDB 8AWG), comparable or higher QED and acceptable SA, and greater novelty than a QED+docking baseline because pharmacophore-focused optimization preserves target-relevant interactions while promoting scaffold diversity.
